title: Implementing a Health Curriculum for the Kupuna Class of Honolulu to Improve Health Literacy: An Evidence-Based Review
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"2arlu","text":"The absence of specialized health
education for seniors may lead to decreased health literacy, increased health
risks, social isolation, and a strain on the healthcare system. Establishing a
health education class could address these issues, improve health outcomes, and
reduce preventable health problems. The review aims to assess the
implementation of a health promotion program to improve the health literacy of
elderly individuals.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ebhs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v7kq","text":"*This protocol follows PRISMA-P guidelines and is provided as an attachment.* ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Jaymie Barnes
